capture.title: Capture
capture.description: Periodically record the focused window and a screenshot, so you can review activity later.
capture.enableDescription: When off, background capture stops (both window activity and screenshots).
capture.screenshotEnableDescription: Only controls screenshots. When off, window activity is still recorded.
capture.intervalDescription: How often window info and screenshots are captured.
capture.intervalHint:
Screenshots trigger when:
• Switching apps → captured immediately
• Browser changes URL → captured immediately
• Same window stays focused for the configured interval → captured
capture.idleDescription: Stop counting time after prolonged mouse/keyboard inactivity.
capture.idleHint:
Detection uses system-level mouse / keyboard / trackpad events.
Set to 0 to disable (never trigger).
capture.dataPathHint: Location of the database and the default screenshots root. Changing requires a restart, and old data must be migrated manually.

workHours.title: Work hours
workHours.description: Only capture during the specified hours, so off-hours activity isn't recorded. Multiple ranges supported.

startup.title: Startup behavior
startup.autoStartDescription: Hindsight starts automatically after you log in.
startup.showWindowDescription: When off, the app stays in the tray and you open it manually when needed.
startup.minimizeToTrayDescriptionMac: Clicking the X in the top-left of the window hides it to the tray; capture and sync keep running in the background. When off, clicking X quits the app.
startup.minimizeToTrayDescriptionWin: Clicking the X in the top-right of the window hides it to the tray; capture and sync keep running in the background. When off, clicking X quits the app.

dataRootDialog.title: Switch data folder?

storage.title: Storage
storage.description: Screenshot retention and current usage. The database is never auto-cleaned.
storage.retentionDescription: Expired screenshot files are deleted automatically (activity records are kept).
storage.modelsPathHint: After changing the path, models in the old folder are not migrated — copy them over manually, or re-download them in the new location.

manage.title: Data management
manage.description: Clean up unwanted data to free disk space.
manage.purgeDbDescription: Delete all window activity records from this device's local database (including mirrored data from other devices). Cloud Drive files are untouched; the next sync will re-mirror peer data. Categories and settings are kept.
manage.purgeCloudDescription: Make **every device** (including this one) forget activities captured on this device before now. Deletes this device's sync files from Google Drive, peers trim their local mirror via tombstone, and this device also trims its own pre-now rows. New activity captured after now keeps syncing normally. Requires being signed in to cloud sync.

purgeDialog.dbMessage: All window activity records in this device's local database (including peer mirrors) will be deleted. The Cloud Drive is untouched; the next sync will automatically re-pull peer mirrors. Categories and settings are unaffected. This cannot be undone.
purgeDialog.shotsMessage: All saved screenshot files will be deleted. The corresponding screenshot references in the database will also be cleared. This cannot be undone.
purgeDialog.cloudMessage: **Every device (including this one)** will forget activities captured on this device before now: this device's sync files on Google Drive are deleted, peers trim their local mirror via tombstone, and this device also trims its own pre-now rows. Activities captured after now keep syncing normally. This cannot be undone.
purgeDialog.cloudDoneMessage: Cloud data cleared: {{count}} Drive file(s) deleted.

browser.title: Browser filter
browser.info: Skip screenshots when the browser address bar URL contains any of these (case-insensitive).

app.title: App filter
app.info: Skip screenshots when the app name or window title contains any of these (case-insensitive).

scope.title: Scope
scope.info: When matched, only screenshots are skipped
scope.noticePrefix: Screenshot filtering is a local-only behavior,
scope.noticeEmph: it does not affect screenshots already saved
scope.noticeMiddle: . To clear historical screenshots, go to

update.title: App updates

status.installing: Downloading and installing — the app will restart when done…

update.autoDescription: When enabled, the app silently checks at startup at the frequency below and notifies you if a new version is available.

timeRangeList.empty: No ranges set — capturing all day

segmentList.empty: No segments yet — click "Add segment" below to start
segmentList.swatchAutoTitle: Auto-gradient based on hour of day

categories.title: App Categories
categories.intro: Group processes into activity categories. Color, icon, name, and bound apps are all customizable.

deleteDialog.title: Delete category
deleteDialog.message: Delete category "{{name}}"? Its apps will be returned to "Other".

pairing.infoTipBody: The same app may have different process names on different systems ("Code" on macOS, "Visual Studio Code" on Windows). Without pairing, time is counted as two separate apps and the category has to be set on each device. Once paired, the name, category, icon, and time are unified.
pairing.instructionSuffix: to move the same app onto one row — unifying name, category, and merged time.
pairing.unassignedHint: Unassigned rows are highlighted in yellow
pairing.noDevices: No device data yet. Check back after capturing for a while.

engine.sectionDesc: Hindsight ships with the llama.cpp inference engine and runs everything locally — screenshots are never uploaded to any external service.

noCuda.headline: NVIDIA CUDA was not detected.
noCuda.prefix: Vision LLMs run very slowly on CPU. We recommend installing the
noCuda.suffix: (≥ 12.4) first; Hindsight will automatically switch to the GPU-accelerated variant after a restart.

engine.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall the local AI engine? Model files will not be affected.
engine.uninstallConfirmMessage: Model files will not be affected.

actions.testTooltipReady: Start the engine (if not running) and verify by hitting the local /v1/models endpoint
actions.releaseVramTooltip: Stop the engine and free GPU memory now. It will spawn again automatically on the next summary run.
actions.openFolderTooltipInstalled: Reveal the install folder in the file manager

engineParams.vramBreakdown: (weights {{weights}} + KV cache {{kv}} + ~2.0 overhead · for {{params}}B model + {{ctx}}K ctx × {{slots}} slot(s))

models.sectionDesc: Vision LLM used for local inference; GGUF files are downloaded from HuggingFace.

cloud.step2Tooltip: Step 2 (segment summary) is routed to the cloud API. Edit endpoint / model in the Cloud API tab.

card.pauseTooltip: Pause the current download. Partial bytes are kept; resume later picks up where you left off.
card.visionTooltip: Supports image input — can run step 1 (image describe) and step 2 (segment summary).
card.textOnlyTooltip: Text-only model — can run step 2 (segment summary) but not step 1.
card.capImageTooltip: Supports image input — eligible for step 1 (image describe).
card.capTextTooltip: Supports text generation — eligible for step 2 (segment summary).
card.step1ToggleOnTooltip: Use this model for the vision step (step 1: image describe). Replaces the previous selection.
card.step1ToggleOffTooltip: Click to clear the vision step (will fall back to the legacy active model).
card.step1DisabledTooltip: This model has no vision capability — can't run the image describe step.
card.step2ToggleOnTooltip: Use this model for the text step (step 2: segment summary). Replaces the previous selection.
card.step2ToggleOffTooltip: Click to clear the text step (will fall back to the legacy active model).

models.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall {{name}}? This will delete the main weights{{extra}} — cannot be undone.
models.uninstallConfirmMessage: This will delete the main weights{{extra}}. The action cannot be undone.

expand.busyTooltip: Can collapse only after downloads finish

custom.hint: Download any GGUF file from a HuggingFace repo. The file must be loadable by llama.cpp; the mmproj field is only needed for vision models.
custom.errorRequired: Repository and main weights file are required.

brief.sectionInfo: Included in AI summaries to help the model understand your work and context.
brief.placeholder: e.g. I'm a backend developer who mostly writes Rust and TypeScript; on weekends I tinker with games.

prompt.sectionDesc: Tell the model how to write summaries. Each language has a built-in default; click Save after editing, or Reset to revert to default.

actions.resetTooltip: Refill the editor with the built-in default (click Save to apply)
actions.saveTooltip: Save the override for the current language

segments.sectionInfo: AI summaries are produced per segment; screenshots within each segment are sampled by similarity before being sent to the model.

filter.rowHint:
Click to toggle:
• Colored + category icon = included in AI analysis
• Gray outline + closed-eye icon = excluded

frame.sectionDesc: Screenshots can pile up over time — deduplicate by similarity first, then send the rest to the model to save time and tokens.
frame.dedupThresholdHint: Merge two screenshots when their cosine similarity ≥ threshold. Higher = more conservative (keep more); lower = more aggressive (merge more, may drop frames you wanted). Default 95% drops ~70% on real-world data.
frame.maxImagesHint: Cap on screenshots sampled per segment after deduplication. Step 1 (vision describe) runs once per image, so high values directly add segment processing time. Default 1024; raise if you want more coverage of long segments.

external.sectionDesc: Generate segment summaries via a cloud model. Screenshots are never uploaded — per-image descriptions stay on your local vision model.
external.enableHint: When on, each segment summary call goes to the API configured below. Step 1 (per-image description) still runs locally.
external.missingFields: Fill in Base URL and Model ID first
external.privacyNote: Note: when enabled, app names, categories, and text descriptions of screenshots are sent to the cloud API on each segment summary call. The images themselves are not uploaded.

devices.title: Devices
devices.meta: Manage this device's name and the sync status of other devices.
devices.emptyOthers: Sign in with the same account on another device to see it here.

desc.signInPrompt: Sign in with Google to sync activity data across devices via Drive. Screenshots are never uploaded.
desc.configurePrompt: Before first use, create an OAuth credential in Google Cloud Console (about 3 minutes).

actions.forceResyncTitle: Reset sync cursor + clear failed queue + re-upload all local data + re-download all cloud data. Use when devices disagree for a long time; may take several minutes.
actions.reauthTitle: Credentials expired — sign in again to refresh the token

confirm.forceResync: Force a full resync? This may take several minutes and use bandwidth. Only run it when devices have disagreed for a while.

toast.forceResyncDone: Resync started: cleared {{cleared}} failed items, re-queued {{enqueued}} days. Push/pull continues in the background — watch progress in the cloud sync panel.

cloud.restartHint: Signed in to a new account. Restart the app to switch to that account's local database.

errors.credExpired: Credentials expired. Please sign in again.
errors.transient: Sync hit a transient error; retrying automatically…

step2.title: Configure the OAuth consent screen
step2.bodyMiddle: → fill in any app name and email → set Audience to External → enter your email again under contact info → click

step3.title: Create the OAuth client
step3.bodyPrefix: Open Clients on the left → click
step3.bodySuffix: at the top → pick Desktop app as the application type → use any name. The Client ID and Client Secret will then be shown.

step4.title: Paste the Client ID and Client Secret below

weekly.title: Weekly
weekly.hint: Weekly activity rollup with date + theme breakdown.
weekly.subtitle: Synthesize this week's daily reports into a single weekly review

actions.startTooltip: Builds a weekly review from this week's existing daily reports — no screenshots, much faster than daily
actions.regenerateTooltip: Regenerating will clear and replace this week's weekly report
actions.noModelTooltip: Pick a summary model in AI Settings → Models, or enable Cloud API
actions.deleteTooltip: Clear this week's weekly report (does not affect daily reports)
actions.deleteEmptyTooltip: No weekly report yet to delete
actions.deleteConfirmMessage: The weekly report content will be cleared. Daily reports are unaffected. This cannot be undone.
actions.exportTooltip: Export the current weekly report as a Markdown file
actions.exportEmptyTooltip: No weekly report to export yet
actions.exportRunningTooltip: Weekly report is generating; export after it finishes

errors.noSummaryModel: Pick a summary model in AI Settings → Models, or enable Cloud API

partialMissing.title: Some days don't have a daily report
partialMissing.message: {{count}} day(s) this week have no daily report: {{dates}}. If you continue, those days will use only their app stats in place of a daily report; the rest will still use the daily reports as usual. Continue?

fullMissing.title: No daily reports for this week
fullMissing.message: None of the 7 days this week has a daily report, but {{activityCount}} of them have app usage records to work from. Generating will only produce a simplified weekly review based on app stats — noticeably lower quality than with daily reports. Continue?
fullMissing.messageNoActivity: None of the 7 days this week has a daily report, and there are no app usage records either. Generation will very likely fail. Try anyway?

body.empty: Click "Generate" above to synthesize a weekly review from this week's daily reports.
body.engineStarting: Loading model — about 30-90s for the first run…
body.summarizing: The model is synthesizing this week's daily reports into a weekly review…

monthly.title: Monthly
monthly.hint: Monthly activity trends and highlights. Feature in development.

daily.subtitle: Per-segment rollup of today's screenshots and app usage, generated by a local vision model.

runtimeMissing.title: AI engine not installed
runtimeMissing.message: An image-embedding model is needed for screenshot deduplication. Together with the vision engine the download is ~80 MB. Generation will resume after download finishes.

actions.startTooltip: The local vision model analyzes each segment in turn; duration depends on hardware (a few seconds per segment on GPU, tens of seconds on CPU).
actions.regenerateTooltip: Regenerating clears all segment summaries for this day and runs them again.
actions.noModelTooltip: Pick a model in AI Settings → Models first.
actions.deleteTooltip: Clear ai_summaries (source=daily) and ai_image_descriptions for this day (cannot be undone).
actions.deleteEmptyTooltip: No daily data yet to delete.
actions.deleteConfirm: Delete daily report data for {{date}}? Segment summaries and per-image descriptions will be cleared. The Debug tab is unaffected. This cannot be undone.
actions.deleteConfirmMessage: Segment summaries and per-image descriptions will be cleared. The Debug tab is unaffected. This cannot be undone.
actions.exportTooltip: Export all segment summaries for the current date as a Markdown file.
actions.exportEmptyTooltip: No segment summaries to export yet.
actions.exportRunningTooltip: Summary in progress — wait until it finishes before exporting.
actions.retryDisabledTooltip: Whole-day summary is running; wait until it finishes or is stopped before retrying.

errors.noVisionModel: Pick a vision model in AI Settings → Models first.

body.empty: Click "Start summary" above to generate this segment.
body.analyzing: Sending screenshots to the model… {{done}} / {{total}} done
body.summarizing: Image descriptions done ({{count}}); model is now writing the segment summary…
body.skipped: No screenshots in this time range (computer was off / everything was on the privacy list / everything was in excluded categories).

export.heading: Daily report — {{dateLabel}} · {{date}}
export.stateSkipped: ⚪ **Skipped** — no screenshots in this time range

chat.subtitle: Chat with the AI about your activity data — search history, check app time, reflect on the day.

empty.title: What would you like to ask?
empty.hint: Try one of the examples below, or just type at the bottom.

search.label: Search history
search.q: Find when I fixed the PairingSection hover bug

stat.label: Stats
stat.q: How long did I spend in Cursor last week?

reflect.label: Reflect
reflect.q: How was my focus today? Give me reflections

search.intro: Found 3 relevant matches in past segment summaries:
search.hit1Snippet: Tweaking the PairingSection hover/glow effect in the Hindsight project. Tried fixed-width grid template vs minmax repeatedly, ended up using :has(.nameCol:hover) to expand the whole column.
search.hit2Snippet: Kept fixing the actionCol left-align + padding-left issue. Discovered 1fr nameCol auto-shrinks to cancel out padding, eventually changed grid template to a fixed 140px.
search.hit3Snippet: First noticed the trigger didn't align to the same X line; suspected grid sub-pixel rounding or flex justify-end was the cause.

stat.intro: Last week Cursor: 12h 23m total, day-by-day breakdown:

reflect.section1Body: About 4 hours of deep focus (2h in the morning + 2h in the afternoon); the rest of the time was context-switching and chatting. Focus was decent overall, but for ~40 minutes in the afternoon app-switching ran high (28 switches/hour).
reflect.section2Body: Afternoon 14:00–16:00 in Cursor with almost no switching — the deepest work block of the day.
reflect.section3Body: After 19:00, social apps took up 60% of the time. Consider pushing those into a focused 30-minute window before bed, leaving a continuous block after dinner for work or reading.

fallback.text:
(mock) This is a sample reply. The backend isn't wired up yet; only a few keyword groups are recognized:
• Search history — keywords like "search / find / when / hover / bug"
• Stats — keywords like "how long / spent / cursor"
• Reflect — keywords like "reflect / focus / today / how was"

maxImagesInfo.tooltip: How many screenshots per segment to send to the model — fewer means faster debugging.

actions.startInfo: Regenerate image descriptions & segment summaries for this day.
actions.describeOnlyInfo: Step 1 only: per-image vision descriptions for today, segment summaries are skipped.
actions.describeOnlyAria: Generate image descriptions only (skip segment summaries)
actions.summarizeOnlyInfo: Step 2 only: rebuild segment summaries from existing image descriptions. Segments with no step-1 data are marked skipped.
actions.summarizeOnlyAria: Generate segment summaries only (skip image descriptions)
actions.clearDescriptionsTooltip: Clear all image descriptions for this day (segment summaries kept, cannot be undone).
actions.clearDescriptionsConfirm: Delete all image descriptions for {{date}}? Segment summaries will be kept. This cannot be undone.
actions.clearDescriptionsConfirmMessage: Segment summaries will be kept. This cannot be undone.
actions.clearSummariesTooltip: Clear all segment summaries for this day (image descriptions kept, cannot be undone).
actions.clearSummariesConfirm: Delete all segment summaries for {{date}}? Image descriptions will be kept. This cannot be undone.
actions.clearSummariesConfirmMessage: Image descriptions will be kept. This cannot be undone.
actions.exportDescriptionsMdTooltip: Export all image descriptions for this day as a Markdown file.
actions.exportDescriptionsMdEmptyTooltip: No image descriptions to export yet.
actions.exportSummariesMdTooltip: Export all segment summaries for this day as a Markdown file.
actions.exportSummariesMdEmptyTooltip: No segment summaries to export yet.
actions.startTooltipReady: Debug mode: re-runs every segment for the day (clears existing results), per-image descriptions stream live.
actions.startTooltipNoModel: Pick a model in AI Settings → Models first.
actions.deleteTooltip: Clear all image descriptions + segment summaries for this day (cannot be undone).
actions.deleteConfirm: Delete all AI artifacts for {{date}}? Segment summaries and per-image descriptions will be cleared. This cannot be undone.
actions.exportTooltip: Export all debug data on this page as a JSON file.

filter.title: Filter
filter.description: Applies only to this debug window — does not change the global AI Settings.
filter.categoriesHint:
Click to toggle:
• Color + category icon = included in AI analysis
• Gray outline + closed-eye icon = excluded

frame.title: Frame sampling
frame.description: Many screenshots in a segment — first deduplicate by similarity, then send to the model to save time and tokens. Applies only to this debug window.
frame.dedupThresholdHint: Merge two screenshots when their cosine similarity ≥ threshold. Higher = more conservative (keep more); lower = more aggressive (merge more, may drop frames you wanted). Default 95% drops ~70% on real-world data.

engine.title: Engine parameters (startup)
engine.description: Changing these stops the engine, restarts with the new parameters, then runs. After debug runs the engine is stopped unconditionally — the next daily summary will lazy-start with defaults, so global state isn't polluted.

errors.noVisionModel: Pick a vision model in AI Settings → Models first.

debug.scopePlaceholder: {{type}} debug pending backend implementation. Switch back to "Daily" to debug the two-step daily flow (per-image descriptions → segment summary).

imagePrompt.title: Per-image describe prompt
imagePrompt.description: Used when the model describes each screenshot independently. Changes apply to this window only.
imagePrompt.label: Prompt

segPrompt.title: Segment summary prompt
segPrompt.description: Used when aggregating per-image descriptions + app stats into a segment summary. Changes apply to this window only.
segPrompt.label: Prompt

cloudApi.title: Cloud API
cloudApi.description: Run segment summaries (step 2) through a cloud model for this run. Endpoint / model / API key are inherited from AI Settings; this toggle only switches the path.
cloudApi.enableHint: Turn off to use local step 2. Configure cloud credentials in AI Settings → Cloud API.

perImage.title: Per-image descriptions
perImage.descriptionBase: step 1 output — each screenshot gets an independent description from the vision LLM
perImage.descriptionSuffix: . Click a filename to preview the original; click "Rerun" at the end of a row to regenerate that image's description with current debug parameters.
perImage.empty: No data yet. Click "Run" above to have the model describe each screenshot.
perImage.retryTooltipBusy: Whole-day summary is running; wait until it finishes or is stopped before rerunning.
perImage.retryTooltipReady: Regenerate this image's description with current debug parameters.

segments.title: Segment summary
segments.descriptionBase: step 2 output — segment summary aggregated from per-image descriptions + app stats
segments.empty: No segment summary yet. Click "Run" above to have the model aggregate per-image descriptions into a segment summary.

events.label: Event stream (rolling 200)
events.empty: No events yet. They will scroll in here once you click Run.

engineLogs.label: Engine log
engineLogs.refreshTooltip: Fetch recent stderr / stdout from the llama-server child process.
engineLogs.copyTooltip: Copy all log lines to the clipboard.
engineLogs.emptyPrefix: No log fetched yet. Click "Refresh" above to fetch once; content appears only after the engine has run a summary. Look for
engineLogs.emptySuffix: lines to tell whether the GPU is being used.
